name: Summer Salad Parisienne
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 2 quarts of water to a boil in a medium-sized pot. Do not salt.
2. Scrub the potatoes and boil until fork tender. Remove from water and cool before dicing.
3. Meanwhile, trim the green beans and steam for 5-7 minutes. Peel, seed, and dice the cucumber.
4. In the bottom of a large salad bowl, whisk the garlic into the mustard until well combined.
5. Pour in the olive oil a little at a time, whisking well after each addition. You will have a smooth, pungent sauce.
6. Add the potatoes, green beans, cucumber, carrot, bell pepper, cabbage, and onion to the bowl and toss well to coat with the dressing.
7. Wash and dry the romaine lettuce and tear into bite-sized pieces. Add to the bowl, along with the herbs and parmesan.
8. Toss again to combine well. Sprinkle black pepper and sesame seeds over everything.
9. Drizzle lemon juice over the salad and toss once more before serving.
Note: This recipe takes well to improvisation. Add whatever you have on hand - cooked beans, nuts, etc. A good suggestion for fresh herbs would be basil, tarragon, and chives.
